facts_disclosed: Dog plan is dead — landlord said absolutely no pets

turns.content: so the dog thing is dead. landlord said absolutely no pets. not even with a deposit.
Oh no. I'm sorry — was this a surprise, or had you been worried about it?

kind of both? I thought my lease said pets were allowed with a deposit but when I actually reread it... there's a clause that says the landlord can deny pets at their discretion, and apparently that discretion is "no." I emailed asking and got back a very flat "no pets of any kind" response. no explanation.

That's such a deflating response to get — especially after you'd already done the research and gotten excited. Just "no pets of any kind" with nothing else attached to it is so cold. Were you close to committing, or had you not gotten that far yet?

I had emailed the breeder and everything. like I was pretty close to putting down a deposit on a puppy. so I had to email her and explain I couldn't do it right now, which felt terrible. she was really nice about it but still

Ugh, that's the worst — you'd gotten to the point where it felt real, contacted the breeder, probably had a name picked out in your head, and then had to walk it back. That's a proper disappointment, not just a minor inconvenience. It's the kind of thing where the letdown is proportional to how excited you'd allowed yourself to get. Are you okay?

I mean... yeah, I'm okay. it's not a tragedy lol. I think what's frustrating is that it was so close to working. like all the conditions lined up except the one that I should have checked first. I work from home, I have time, I've thought about this for a year. and then the lease says no

The "closest thing to working" frustrations really do hit differently than the clear-no situations. It's easy to accept something that was never possible, but something that was almost possible and fell on the last technicality — that's genuinely aggravating. And you're right that you had all the real conditions sorted. This wasn't about you not being ready. It was a lease clause. Is there any part of you that's tempted to push back on the landlord, or is it clearly a closed door?

it feels pretty closed. I don't have a great relationship with my landlord anyway, like it's fine but not warm. I don't think appealing is going to go anywhere. honestly my first thought was "maybe I'll just do it anyway" but that's obviously a terrible idea

That first thought is very human and also yes, genuinely a bad idea — an unauthorized pet risks lease termination, which is a much bigger disruption than a delayed puppy plan. You already know this. The silver lining, if there is one: you mentioned you're moving at some point anyway, right? The lease situation was already something you were thinking about. A new place with an explicit pet-friendly clause in writing — not just "deposit allowed, landlord discretion" but actually pet-friendly — changes the whole picture.

yeah, my lease is actually up end of august so I'm moving anyway. I've been apartment hunting. so the plan is basically... maybe after I move. if the new place allows pets and I've vetted that properly this time

Okay, that actually reframes the whole thing — this isn't "the dog plan is dead," it's "the dog plan is delayed two months." If you're actively apartment hunting, you can make pet-friendly a hard filter on your search. Not "allows pets with deposit at discretion" but verified, explicitly, in writing. A lot of listings will say pet-friendly and mean it, versus the language your current place has which is basically a landlord trap. Ask prospective landlords directly: "Is this actually pet-friendly or is it at your discretion?" Some buildings have breed/size restrictions, some have monthly pet fees — get specific. If you land a truly pet-friendly place in September, the timeline barely shifts.
that's true. I think I'm mourning a specific puppy from a specific litter more than the whole concept lol. like that litter is gone now

That's really what it is, isn't it — it's the specific loss, the specific imagined dog that you'd almost picked, not the abstract concept of dog ownership. That's a real small grief and it makes sense to feel it. But litters come around, breeders have waiting lists, and if you find a truly pet-friendly place in August or September, you could have a puppy by late fall if you wanted. The Round Rock breeder — do you have a sense of whether she has litters regularly, or was this a rare thing?
she mentioned she usually has two litters a year. so there would probably be another one in the fall or winter. I might just stay on her radar and circle back once I have a new place sorted

That's a genuinely good plan — drop her a quick note saying something like "circumstances changed, I'm moving soon and once I'm settled in a pet-friendly place I'd love to be on your list for a future litter." Most good breeders appreciate that kind of honesty and keeping-in-touch approach. It keeps the door open without committing to anything. And it gives you a clear next step rather than just a dead end. Two months from now you could be in a new apartment with a pet-friendly lease, actively on a wait list.
okay. this helped actually. I came in feeling deflated and now I have like a next step. "find pet-friendly place, email breeder in fall" is a real plan
That's a real plan. The delay is annoying but the dream is still intact. Go find yourself a place with good natural light, updated HVAC, and a lease that actually says pets welcome.
